news.title: Lucknow: LDA to draft GIS-based master plan 2051 for six districts included in state capital region
news.para: Lucknow Development Authority is preparing a GIS-based Master Plan-2051 for the Uttar Pradesh State Capital Region. This digital plan will integrate land use and development projects across six districts on one platform. The regional master plan will define land use, settlement patterns, and transport networks for planned growth. It proposes new highways, rail links, and mass transit systems for coordinated development.

news.title: Gurugram: A decade after moving in, 206 Unitech Fresco families get OCs for their flats
news.para: Unitech Fresco residents in Gurgaon finally received occupation certificates for their homes.  This long-awaited approval brings legal certainty to 206 families living in four towers.  The certificates were issued by the town and country planning department after extensive verification processes.  Residents expressed immense relief after years of facing complications with property registrations and loans.  This development marks the resolution of a significant pending issue for the society.

news.title: Sebi exempts Mehta Family Trust from Saurashtra Cement open offer
news.para: Sebi exempted Mehta Family Trust from an open offer for Saurashtra Cement shares.  This exemption allows internal family reorganisation and succession planning.  Jay Mahendra Mehta and Juhi Chawla Mehta are trustees of the family trust.  The transaction involves indirect acquisition of shares and voting rights.  This exemption is valid for one year from the order date.

news.title: Chennai developers invest over ₹2,500 crore in land acquisitions in two years
news.para: Developers invested over ₹2,500 crore in Chennai land parcels for future projects. Major transactions included Baashyaam Group's acquisition on Haddows Road for ₹1,212 crore. Joint ventures and other firms also secured significant land in Padi and Velachery. This buying spree indicates a strategy shift towards established and emerging suburban markets. Developers are focusing on redevelopment potential and scale in fast-growing corridors.

news.title: Pradeep Constructions challenges GHMC showcause notice in Telangana HC
news.para: Pradeep Constructions challenged a GHMC notice proposing building permission cancellation. The firm argues the notice is illegal and based on unverified material. GHMC issued the notice alleging construction within Hussainsagar Lake's full tanl level. The developer obtained permissions in 2018 and revised them in 2020. Hundreds of homebuyers face irreparable loss if permissions are cancelled.

news.title: Tamil Nadu government appoints interim panel to lead RERA
news.para: The Tamil Nadu government has appointed an interim panel to lead the Real Estate Regulatory Authority. Retired IAS officer K Phanindra Reddy will chair the new interim panel. This action follows the recent resignations of the authority's previous chairperson and members. The state government has superseded TNRERA for six months or until its reconstitution. The interim panel will now perform all statutory functions and duties of the authority.

news.title: SC transfers money-laundering case involving Amit Katyal to Delhi
news.para: The Supreme Court moved Amit Katyal's money-laundering case to Delhi. This case involves alleged cheating of homebuyers in Gurugram.  The Enforcement Directorate arrested Katyal last year in this matter.  Investigations revealed fraudulent bookings and fund diversion by his company.  This action follows allegations of duping buyers of hundreds of crores.

news.title: NoBroker targets 30% revenue growth in FY27, eyes profitability in 12-15 months
news.para: NoBroker expects to grow revenue by about 30% in FY27 and become fully profitable in the next 12-15 months. The company said real estate continues to contribute about 50% of its revenue, while primary sales, NoBrokerHood, home services and packers & movers are supporting its broader ecosystem strategy.

news.title: Signature Global pre-sales decline 25% to ₹1,970 crore in Q1 FY27
news.para: Signature Global recorded pre-sales of ₹1,970 crore in Q1 FY27, down 25% year-on-year from ₹2,640 crore in Q1 FY26. Its average sales realisation increased to ₹17,093 per sq ft during the quarter.

news.title: CBI files sixteenth chargesheet against Noida builder, bank officials
news.para: The CBI filed its sixteenth chargesheet concerning alleged large-scale homebuyers' fraud cases.  A Noida-based real estate company and bank officials face accusations of conspiracy.  These individuals allegedly cheated buyers and investors through fraudulent and deceptive means.  The investigation has unearthed substantial evidence of illegal activities and financial impropriety.  The agency continues to probe thirty-three other similar cases across the country.

news.title: Beyond buildings: How architects are shaping sustainability and policy today
news.para: Architects now contribute significantly to India's development and urban planning.  Their interdisciplinary knowledge enhances their influence on decision-making processes.  Growing public demand for quality spaces expands the architect's professional scope.  Sustainability and policy development are key areas where architects offer expertise.  This evolving role positions architecture to shape India's future settlements.

news.title: SC dismisses plea challenging CRZ clearance for two new floors at Shah Rukh Khan's Mannat
news.para: The Supreme Court dismissed a petition challenging construction clearance for Shah Rukh Khan's residence.  The court questioned the petitioner's motives and refused to interfere with the NGT's order.  The National Green Tribunal had previously dismissed the plea alleging procedural errors in the clearance.  The tribunal found no irregularities in the approval granted by the coastal authority.  This decision provides relief to the actor regarding his Mumbai home's additional floors.

news.title: As India's energy revolution accelerates, real estate stands to gain
news.para: Energy access is shifting from security to national sovereignty for nations. Real estate infrastructure converts energy potential into sustained economic value for India. Industrial parks and renewable energy facilities create economic epicenters and ecosystems. India's policy architecture supports energy ambition and infrastructure execution. Real estate is the ground for India's energy sovereignty and global standing.

news.title: Rajasthan: 86 colonies plan mahapanchayat over pending land titles
news.para: Residents of 86 colonies in Sanganer and Bagru are demanding land titles.  These families established their homes decades ago through cooperative societies.  They warn of boycotting local body elections if demands are unmet.  A mahapanchayat and public movement are planned if no decision occurs.  The Niyaman Hetu Sangharsh Samiti leads this ongoing community effort.

news.title: Mumbai: BMC plans to launch single portal for construction waste monitoring by July-end
news.para: Mumbai's BMC will launch an integrated construction and demolition waste management portal by July.  This new system aims to monitor all waste generated within the city.  A soil exchange platform will also be introduced for developers and contractors.  Government agencies and infrastructure projects will register on this single digital platform.  The portal will prevent illegal dumping and ensure waste accountability across Mumbai.

news.title: Andhra Pradesh government approves Visakhapatnam's master plan 2041
news.para: The Andhra Pradesh government has approved the revised VMRDA Master Plan-2041. This plan incorporates several key development proposals for regional growth. It includes major infrastructure initiatives and economic development projects. The plan also integrates environmental safeguards and connectivity improvements. This framework guides the Visakhapatnam Metropolitan Region's future development until 2041.

news.title: Panchkula civic body flags ₹37 crore property tax dues
news.para: Panchkula Municipal Corporation identified property tax arrears totaling Rs 37.68 crore.  These outstanding dues are from 697 properties with payments exceeding two lakh rupees.  Private properties owe Rs 25.95 crore, while government entities owe Rs 11.73 crore.  Despite a waiver, only Rs 5.92 crore was recovered by the civic body.  A 75% interest rebate is now offered until August 31.

news.title: Kolkata: Only 11 out of 136 high-rise sites surveyed get construction go-ahead
news.para: A government committee has cleared only eleven of 136 surveyed buildings.  Many projects remain stalled due to non-compliance with labor registration requirements.  Officials are inspecting worker facilities and safety procedures at construction sites.  Developers often shift responsibility for labor compliance to contractors.  The Credai will urge developers to adhere to labor laws for project clearance.

news.title: Why 24,000 flats not offered to slum dwellers facing eviction near PM residence: Delhi HC
news.para: Delhi High Court questioned authorities about relocating slum dwellers 45 kms away. Over 24,000 constructed homes for evictees remain unused and in disrepair. The court demanded answers on why these vacant houses are not available. Authorities spent nearly Rs 2,000 crore on these unused rehabilitation units. The court emphasized the rights of petitioners under Article 21.

news.title: MHADA lottery on hold... for want of a VIP
news.para: MHADA homebuyers face prolonged uncertainty as the lottery draw date remains unannounced.  The housing authority is still trying to finalize a senior political leader as chief guest.  This delay has caused significant anxiety for thousands of hopeful applicants.  MHADA has already extended application deadlines and postponed the draw multiple times.  The authority plans to announce the lottery date soon, even without a minister.
